title: Associate
description:
About the Role

We are looking for a Private Wealth / Private Client Associate (1–4 PQE) to join our Singapore private wealth practice group.

This role is ideal for an associate who is interested in high-end private wealth work, enjoys working closely with founders, families and next-generation clients, and wants to develop into a trusted private wealth adviser, rather than a purely technical executor.

The successful candidate will work on complex and cross-border private wealth matters, including trust and estate planning, family governance, succession planning, and wealth structuring for ultra-high-net-worth individuals and families across Asia.

Key Responsibilities

• Advise high-net-worth individuals and families on private wealth structuring, including trusts, foundations, holding structures, and succession arrangements

• Assist with the establishment, restructuring, and administration of trusts and other wealth planning vehicles, including cross-border structures

• Support partner on estate planning, succession planning, and family governance matters, including wills, family charters, and inter-generational planning

• Draft and review private wealth documentation, including trust deeds, supplemental deeds, letters of wishes, deeds of appointment and retirement, and related agreements

• Conduct legal research and prepare technical memoranda and client advice on private wealth, trust, and inheritance law issues

• Liaise with clients, trustees, family offices, tax advisers, and other professional advisers across jurisdictions

• Assist with cross-border private wealth matters, including coordination with foreign counsel on multi-jurisdictional structures

• Support the team in developing client relationships and long-term advisory work, with exposure to business development and client strategy discussions

Qualifications and Requirements

• Common law / PRC-qualified with 1–4 years of post-qualification experience

• Prior experience or strong interest in private wealth / private client / trust / estate planning work (experience in a reputable law firm preferred)

• Strong drafting and analytical skills, with attention to detail and commercial awareness

• Ability to handle sensitive and confidential family matters with discretion and maturity

• Good understanding of, or willingness to develop expertise in, cross-border private wealth structures

• Chinese language skills (written or conversational) are an advantage, particularly forcommunicating with Chinese-speaking clients and reviewing Chinese-language materials

• Strong interpersonal skills, sound judgment, and a collaborative, client-focused approach

Why Join Us

• Exposure to high-quality, complex private wealth work rather than routine or commoditized matters

• Direct partner mentorship and close involvement in client advisory work, not just document production

• Opportunity to develop into a trusted adviser to founders, families, and family offices

• Involvement in cross-border and Asia-focused private wealth structures

• A professional, entrepreneurial, and collegiate team with clear long-term growth prospects

districts.location: D01 Cecil, Marina, People’s Park, Raffles Place
